[2020-11-29 15:06] terrareveene (Terra Reveene): As an example: If a set contains a lot of really good red rush units, then P2 might be favored, so the randomizer might add a decent earlyish blue defender to give P1 more options, etc.

[2020-11-29 15:09] masn6811 (Masne98888): but you see, that also makes the game samey

[2020-11-29 15:11] masn6811 (Masne98888): you're hiding details under the rug

[2020-11-29 15:11] masn6811 (Masne98888): elegance is when your solution is so simple you can tell them all the details
[2020-11-29 15:11] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): an alternative is adding more "counter"/"stabilizing" units into the pool.
[2020-11-29 15:12] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): we have different definitions of elegance
[2020-11-29 15:12] terrareveene (Terra Reveene): And, to be clear, when I say "corresponding Y quality" I mean that it could be something like "if there's a "Red rush unit" in the set, make sure there's one of "early defender", "alternative red producer", "green/blue rush unit", etc. In the set as well"
[2020-11-29 15:14] terrareveene (Terra Reveene): And, yeah, it'd require quite a bit of work. But it seems to me like most of the sets that are problematic are caused by there being units of quality X that favor Player A, and no units of quality Y that favors Player B
